title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Rosamond Darnley confronts Linda Marshall regarding her intense and troubling feelings about Elena's death. When Rosamond suggests that a murderer must have sneaked onto the island to kill Elena, Linda reveals that her father previously married a woman who was tried for murder, implying her father might not view murder as entirely wrong. Rosamond denies this, asserting that the police have an unshakable alibi for Linda's father, prompting Linda to run back to her hotel.

Hercule Poirot questions Christine Redfern about Linda Marshall’s movements, noting a discrepancy in Christine's previous statement that Linda had gone swimming. Christine admits she was surprised by Linda's claim because Linda was carrying a package of candles; when the packaging broke, Linda appeared uneasy and embarrassed. Poirot also inquires about a bright green tear-off calendar that Christine recalls seeing. Furthermore, Poirot shows Christine a book titled *Witchcraft and the History of Invisible Poison*, which Christine remembers seeing Linda quickly hide in a village bookstore. After confirming that Christine did not use her bathtub that morning, Poirot moves on.

Poirot later approaches Mrs. Gardiner, who is distracted by a jigsaw puzzle due to the tension of the inquest. Poirot assists her by correctly placing a puzzle piece, explaining that the tip of a black cat's tail is white. When Mrs. Gardiner expresses a desire to understand his investigative methods, Poirot compares his process to assembling a mosaic where every odd-shaped piece must fit perfectly.
